questions.question: Why does the vision encoder produce tokens that the text decoder cannot consume directly?
options: They are in a different file format
Image tokens live in a basis the encoder learned during vision pretraining with no correspondence to the decoder's word vectors
questions.explanation: The two encoders learn independent bases; a bridge module is required to align them in a shared space.

questions.question: Why is the two-layer MLP enough for modality alignment in practice?
options: One non-linear bend (GELU) between two linear projections is empirically enough to align CLIP-style features with text embeddings
questions.explanation: LLaVA and its descendants ship this two-layer MLP because the non-linearity fixes curvature mismatches a single linear cannot.

questions.question: Why is the vision encoder frozen during this alignment stage?
options: The 86M-parameter encoder cannot be retrained on a small mock corpus; the 1.3M-parameter projection alone is light enough to align in minutes
PyTorch does not support training the encoder
questions.explanation: Freezing the encoder and text table makes the projection the only thing learning, which is the operational shape of every adapter-based VLM.

questions.question: What does cosine_alignment_loss(image_emb, text_emb) return when the two vectors point in opposite directions?
questions.explanation: Loss is 1 - cos(angle). Antiparallel vectors have cos = -1, so the loss is 1 - (-1) = 2.0.

questions.question: Why does CLS pooling get used to produce a single image-level vector from 197 tokens?
options: Captions are one vector per sample so the image side also needs one vector; the CLS token is the encoder's built-in image summary
questions.explanation: Alignment is one image vector against one caption vector; CLS pooling produces that image vector with no extra parameters.

questions.question: Which production system maps most directly to the lesson 60 pattern?
options: LLaVA 1.5: frozen vision encoder, frozen LLM, train only a two-layer MLP projection
questions.explanation: LLaVA's stage-one training is exactly this: a frozen encoder and LM with a two-layer MLP bridge as the sole trainable piece.
